articles.title: Dozens of dust devils hidden in plain sight
articles.summary: The European Space Agency’s Mars Express has captured part of Mars’s Mamers Valles: a fascinating valley system speckled with brief, tornado-like whirlwinds known as dust devils.

articles.title: One robotic arm to rule them all
Low Water at San Carlos Reservoir
articles.summary: Drought and water releases drained the Arizona reservoir to levels that have led to widespread fish deaths.

articles.summary:

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch 3 Block 2 BlueBird satellites for AST SpaceMobile
articles.summary: The mission is third time SpaceX will launch satellites for AST SpaceMobile and its first of the Block 2 iteration of the BlueBird satellites. Liftoff from pad 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station is scheduled for 2:39 a.m. EDT (0639 UTC).

articles.title: NASA’s Webb Catches Exoplanet Getting Roasted
articles.summary: One well-done gas giant, coming right up! That’s the latest from researchers analyzing NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope’s observations of HD 80606 b, an exoplanet four times the mass of Jupiter with an extremely elliptical orbit that sweeps close by its Sun-like star. The research team is presenting their study and preliminary findings Tuesday at […]

articles.title: Astrobotic unveils Griffin-1 lunar lander
articles.summary:
Astrobotic showed off the lunar lander it plans to launch later this year that will be the vanguard of NASA’s new lunar base ambitions.

articles.title: Astronaut Jessica Meir Assists With Hardware Updates for NASA’s Cold Atom Lab
articles.summary: Description NASA astronaut Jessica Meir inspects optical fibers while installing hardware updates to the agency’s Cold Atom Lab, or CAL, aboard the International Space Station on May 8, 2026. About the size of a minifridge and operated from Earth, CAL chills atoms to temperatures below minus 459 degrees Fahrenheit (minus 273.15 degrees Celsius), so close […]

articles.title: Arianespace CEO Says Strike Will Not Affect Ariane 6 Launch
articles.summary:
A strike that saw entrances to the Guiana Space Centre barricaded has ended, with Arianespace CEO David Cavaillolès saying the labour dispute has not affected tomorrow’s planned Ariane 6 launch from the site. Radio-Télé Péyi Guyane reported that the UTG, or Union of Guyanese Workers, had filed an indefinite general strike notice on 14 June, […]
The post Arianespace CEO Says Strike Will Not Affect Ariane 6 Launch appeared first on European Spaceflight.
